key_insight: EchoRisk introduces the first multicentre, longitudinal echocardiography benchmark with explicit cardiotoxicity labels to support automated risk assessment in cardio-oncology.
clinical_relevance: It could help standardize early detection of therapy-induced cardiac dysfunction during breast cancer treatment, where cardiotoxicity can interrupt cancer therapy.
method: 422 patients from 5 European sites in the CARDIOCARE prospective study
2,159 echocardiography videos across 1,123 exams, with up to 5 timepoints per patient
Three tasks: LVEF estimation, LV dysfunction classification, and baseline-only early cardiotoxicity prediction
evidence_signal: The benchmark includes predefined evaluation protocols and metrics, with baseline R(2+1)D+LSTM models showing strong performance on functional assessment and LV dysfunction classification but weak early prediction from baseline imaging alone.
watch_item: Early cardiotoxicity prediction from a single pre-therapy echocardiogram remains unresolved, so baseline results should not be treated as clinically reliable.
paper_title: EchoRisk: A Multicentre Echocardiography Dataset and Benchmark for Cardio-Oncology
